# local_ancestry_truth { #tspaint.local_ancestry_truth }

```python
tspaint.local_ancestry_truth(ts)
```

True local ancestry per sample as source-population tracts.

For each sample and each marginal tree, climb to the sample's census ancestor
and read its population. Consecutive trees with the same source are merged.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**ts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Tree sequence from :func:`simulate_admixture` (must carry census nodes).

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[**tracts**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sample node id -> list of ``(left, right, population_id)`` covering ``[0, sequence_length)`` with no gaps or overlaps. ``population_id == -1`` marks a sample with no census ancestor (should not occur in this scenario).

<code>[**pop_name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [str](`str`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Population id -> name, for readability.
